title: rhinanthus grandiflorus subsp. angustifolius
intro: Rhinanthus grandiflorus subsp. angustifolius, also known as the narrow-leaved rye-grass of ghosts, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. This hemiparasitic plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Rhinanthus grandiflorus subsp. angustifolius include its use as a natural remedy for gastrointestinal issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to fully understand its therapeutic potential.
